evals.prompt: Write specs for a RegisterAuthor command that appends AuthorRegistered and has a validator requiring a non-empty name. Cover the happy path and the validation failure.
evals.expected_output: In-process specs using CommandScenario<RegisterAuthor>: a happy-path class asserting ShouldBeSuccessful() and ShouldHaveAppendedEvent<RegisterAuthor, AuthorRegistered>(...), and a failure class asserting BOTH ShouldNotBeSuccessful() and ShouldHaveValidationErrors(). Files wrapped in #if DEBUG, under when_registering/.
assertions: Uses CommandScenario<TCommand> (not ChronicleOutOfProcessFixture or an HTTP client)
Happy path asserts ShouldBeSuccessful() and an appended-event assertion
Validation failure asserts BOTH ShouldNotBeSuccessful() and ShouldHaveValidationErrors()
No assertions on validation message strings
Spec files are wrapped in #if DEBUG ... #endif
Folder is when_<verb>, files are and_<condition>.cs, methods are should_<result>

evals.prompt: Write a spec proving a duplicate author name is rejected by the uniqueness constraint.
evals.expected_output: An EventScenario spec: Establish appends an existing AuthorRegistered, Because appends a second with the same name, assertions use ShouldBeFailed() and ShouldHaveConstraintViolationFor(<constraint name>).
assertions: Uses EventScenario for the constraint (not CommandScenario)
Asserts the constraint NAME, not a message string
